title: MCP Is Becoming a Cross-Platform Standard Rather Than a Single-Vendor Connector
intent: Anchor agent integration strategy in an open standard that reduces connector rework and vendor dependency.

source.title: Donating the Model Context Protocol and establishing the Agentic AI Foundation

fact.primary_stat: Anthropic said on December 9, 2025 that MCP had grown to more than 10,000 active public servers.
supporting_stats: Anthropic said MCP had been adopted by ChatGPT, Cursor, Gemini, Microsoft Copilot, and Visual Studio Code.
The company said enterprise-grade MCP deployment support existed across AWS, Cloudflare, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.
Anthropic donated MCP to the Linux Foundation’s Agentic AI Foundation to keep it open, community-driven, and vendor-neutral.

business_relevance: An open integration standard is now strong enough to be a serious architecture choice for agent ecosystems.
Connector work can increasingly be treated as reusable infrastructure instead of app-by-app reinvention.
dzmitryi_commentary: This is exactly the kind of signal that supports an in-house agentic capability strategy. If the protocol layer is open and broadly adopted, the business can invest in reusable context, tools, workflows, and guardrails without binding the operating model to one assistant vendor or one platform runtime.

provenance.note: Extracted and curated by Dzmitryi Kharlanau; enriched for attribution and crawler indexing.

meta.summary: Anchor agent integration strategy in an open standard that reduces connector rework and vendor dependency.
